vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1553

plan.title: release: document default-branch env bypass leak into nested tests

narratives.Description: Release operators can accidentally scope DEFT_ALLOW_DEFAULT_BRANCH_COMMIT too broadly when running the full release workflow. This story documents the safer typed-policy path and pins guidance so nested tests and temporary repos do not inherit the emergency bypass.
narratives.Overview:
## Summary

During the v0.43.0 release attempt, using `DEFT_ALLOW_DEFAULT_BRANCH_COMMIT=1` for the entire `task release` command let the release branch guard pass, but the environment variable leaked into nested `task ci:local` / Go installer tests.

That caused `TestWriteConsumerGitHooks_VendoredCommitBlocked_RealGit` to fail because the vendored test repo inherited the bypass and allowed a direct `master` commit that the test expected the hook to block.

## Why this matters

Future release operators may choose the env-var bypass because the release skill currently names it as an emergency escape hatch for default-branch release commits. For a full release command, that is too broad: it affects child processes and isolated temp repos, producing a false release-preflight failure before any release mutation.

## Desired outcome

Document and/or harden the release workflow so future releases are smoother:

- Prefer the typed direct-commit policy opt-out for release sessions instead of wrapping the whole `task release` invocation in `DEFT_ALLOW_DEFAULT_BRANCH_COMMIT=1`.
- Warn that the env-var bypass is process-wide and can leak into nested tests or temp repos.
- If the env-var path remains documented, scope it only to narrow branch-guard probes, not to `task release` / `task ci:local`.
- Add content coverage in `tests/content/test_release_workflow.py` so the release skill preserves this guidance.

## Observed failure

`task release -- 0.43.0 --summary ...` stopped at Step 5, before any release mutation:

- preflight git status: OK
- branch == master: OK
- vBRIEF lifecycle sync: OK
- tag availability: OK
- CI preflight: FAIL

Failing test:

`TestWriteConsumerGitHooks_VendoredCommitBlocked_RealGit`

Reason: inherited `DEFT_ALLOW_DEFAULT_BRANCH_COMMIT=1` caused the vendored pre-commit hook to allow a direct `master` commit.

## Recovery used

No rollback was needed because the pipeline stopped before changelog promotion, tagging, pushing, or release creation. The tree stayed clean and `v0.43.0` did not exist as a tag or GitHub release.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Update the skills/deft-directive-release/SKILL.md file and docs/RELEASING.md file so release operators prefer typed direct-commit policy opt-out instead of wrapping the full task release command in DEFT_ALLOW_DEFAULT_BRANCH_COMMIT=1.
2. Add pytest verification evidence in tests/content/test_release_workflow.py so the release workflow guidance warns that the env-var bypass is process-wide and must not leak into task ci:local, nested tests, or temporary repos.
narratives.UserStory: As a release operator, I want default-branch bypass guidance scoped to the release guard only, so that nested tests and temporary repos do not inherit an emergency commit bypass.
narratives.Traces: Issue #1553 release failure report; skills/deft-directive-release/SKILL.md release workflow guidance; tests/content/test_release_workflow.py.

items.title: Release guidance prefers typed policy opt-out

narrative.Acceptance: Given an operator needs to release from the default branch, when they read the release skill or release docs, then the guidance prefers the typed direct-commit policy opt-out over wrapping the full task release command in DEFT_ALLOW_DEFAULT_BRANCH_COMMIT=1.

items.title: Process-wide env bypass warning is explicit

narrative.Acceptance: Given DEFT_ALLOW_DEFAULT_BRANCH_COMMIT remains documented, when the release workflow mentions it, then the text warns that the env var is process-wide and can leak into task ci:local, nested tests, and temporary repositories.

items.title: Content tests pin release bypass guidance

narrative.Acceptance: Given tests/content/test_release_workflow.py runs, then it fails if the release workflow drops the typed-policy preference or the warning against broad DEFT_ALLOW_DEFAULT_BRANCH_COMMIT scoping.

expected_outputs: release workflow content tests assert typed-policy guidance and process-wide env bypass warnings

references.title: Issue #1553: release: document default-branch env bypass leak into nested tests
